title: A small box I keep
summary: Describe a small box you keep and what is inside it.
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about a small box you keep. Describe the box itself and say what is inside and why those things are important to you. Use present simple and simple adjectives.
hintStarters: I have a small box in my room…
Inside the box, there is / there are…
The most important thing in the box is…
sampleText: I have a small tin box in my room in Amsterdam. The lid is dark blue and has a simple flower pattern on it. I got this box as a gift when I was twelve years old. Inside the box, there are several small things. There is an old coin from my first trip to Morocco. There is also a paper note with the address of a restaurant in Paris. I ate there with my best friend on our first trip together. At the bottom of the box, there is a faded photo of my grandmother and me in her garden. It is the only photo I have of us together. I also keep a short ribbon from a present my aunt gave me. All these things are small, but they are very precious to me. I open the box two or three times a year and I look at everything inside. Each item reminds me of a happy moment. The box sits on my shelf next to my books.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: two or three times a year
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: hai hay ba lần mỗi năm

sampleAnnotations.phrase: each item reminds me of a happy moment
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: mỗi vật nhắc tôi nhớ một khoảnh khắc vui

sampleAnnotations.phrase: on my shelf next to my books
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: trên kệ bên cạnh sách của tôi

sampleGrammarNotes.title: There is / There are for listing contents
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'there is' và 'there are' để liệt kê những gì có bên trong: 'There is an old coin', 'There are several small things'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'there is' for single items and 'there are' for multiple items: 'There is an old coin', 'There are several small things inside'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Simple adjectives before nouns
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Tính từ đặt trước danh từ: 'a small tin box', 'a faded photo', 'an old coin', 'a short ribbon'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Adjectives come before the noun they describe: 'a small tin box', 'a faded photo', 'an old coin from Morocco'.

sampleTranslationVi: Tôi có một hộp thiếc nhỏ trong phòng ở Amsterdam. Nắp hộp màu xanh đậm và có họa tiết hoa đơn giản. Tôi nhận được hộp này như một món quà khi tôi mười hai tuổi. Bên trong hộp có nhiều thứ nhỏ. Có một đồng xu cũ từ chuyến đi đầu tiên của tôi đến Maroc. Còn có một mảnh giấy ghi địa chỉ của một nhà hàng ở Paris. Tôi ăn ở đó cùng người bạn thân nhất trong chuyến đi đầu tiên của chúng tôi. Ở đáy hộp có một tấm ảnh đã phai màu của bà nội và tôi trong khu vườn của bà. Đó là tấm ảnh duy nhất tôi có của chúng tôi cùng nhau. Tôi cũng giữ một sợi ruy băng ngắn từ món quà dì tặng tôi. Tất cả những thứ này nhỏ thôi, nhưng rất quý giá với tôi. Tôi mở hộp hai hay ba lần mỗi năm và nhìn ngắm mọi thứ bên trong. Mỗi vật nhắc tôi nhớ một khoảnh khắc vui. Hộp đặt trên kệ bên cạnh sách của tôi.

mcQuestions.explanation: 'I have a small tin box in my room in Amsterdam'.
mcQuestions.hint: The city is in the first sentence.

mcQuestions.prompt: What colour is the lid of the box?
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the colour of the lid.

mcQuestions.prompt: Where did the old coin come from?
options: From the writer's first trip to Morocco.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'an old coin from my first trip to Morocco'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Who is in the faded photo?
options: The writer and their best friend.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'a faded photo of my grandmother and me in her garden'.

mcQuestions.prompt: How often does the writer open the box?
options: Two or three times a year.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I open the box two or three times a year'.

options: There is several small things inside.
There is an old coin in the box.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'there is' matches a singular noun 'an old coin'.
mcQuestions.hint: Match 'there is' with a singular noun.

options: Very important and valuable to you.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'precious' means having great personal value or importance.

mcQuestions.prompt: What was written on the paper note inside the box?
options: The phone number of a friend.
The address of a restaurant in Paris.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'a paper note with the address of a restaurant in Paris'.
mcQuestions.hint: A city and a type of place are named.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which adjective fits: 'a ___ photo' (one that has lost colour)?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'faded' means having lost brightness or colour over time.
mcQuestions.hint: Old photos often look this way.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'remind' mean in 'Each item reminds me of a happy moment'?
options: To make you think of something from the past.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'remind' means to bring a past memory or thought to mind.

options: On the shelf next to the books.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'The box sits on my shelf next to my books'.
mcQuestions.hint: The last sentence names the location.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do people keep small objects that have no money value but are very important to them personally?
